hawkish_phrases: does not intend to tighten monetary policy until there is clear evidence that significant progress is being made in eliminating spare capacity
the existing stance of monetary policy remains appropriate
CPI inflation is expected to be around 2% in two years' time
dovish_phrases: CPI inflation is expected to fall further below the 2% target and average around ¼% in the latter part of the year
unemployment rate is projected to rise materially, to around 7½% by the end of the year
a margin of spare capacity is likely to remain until the end of next year
risks to the outlook for GDP are judged to be skewed to the downside
stands ready to adjust monetary policy accordingly to meet its remit
summary: The MPC voted unanimously to hold Bank Rate at 0.1% and maintain its £745 billion asset purchase programme, with clear dovish bias as inflation is expected to fall to around ¼% and unemployment to rise materially to 7½% amid significant spare capacity.
